 {"id":"bd-2d2v","title":"Cross-Terminal Clipboard Abstraction","description":"# Cross-Terminal Clipboard Abstraction

## Background & Motivation

Clipboard access varies across terminals and platforms:
- OSC 52 (modern terminals)
- pbcopy/pbpaste (macOS)
- xclip/xsel (X11)
- wl-copy/wl-paste (Wayland)

**The solution:**
- Unified Clipboard API
- Auto-detect best method
- Graceful fallback chain

**Relationship to existing beads:**
- Requires bd-10i.4.4 (TerminalCapabilities detection)
- Extends bd-2rs (OSC 52 implementation)

## Technical Design

```rust
pub struct Clipboard {
    backend: ClipboardBackend,
}

pub enum ClipboardBackend {
    Osc52,
    MacOS,  // pbcopy/pbpaste
    X11,    // xclip
    Wayland, // wl-copy
    Unavailable,
}

pub enum ClipboardError {
    NotAvailable,
    WriteError(String),
    ReadError(String),
}

impl Clipboard {
    pub fn detect(caps: &TerminalCapabilities) -> Self {
        // Try OSC 52 first if terminal supports it
        // Fall back to platform-specific tools
    }
    
    pub fn set(&self, text: &str) -> Result<(), ClipboardError> { ... }
    pub fn get(&self) -> Result<String, ClipboardError> { ... }
}
```

## Logging Requirements

```rust
// Backend detected (INFO level)
tracing::info!(backend = ?backend, "Clipboard backend detected");

// Clipboard write (DEBUG level)
tracing::debug!(backend = ?backend, bytes = text.len(), "Clipboard write");

// Fallback triggered (WARN level)
tracing::warn!(
    primary = ?primary_backend,
    fallback = ?fallback_backend,
    reason = reason,
    "Clipboard fallback triggered"
);
```

## E2E Tests

```bash
# Test clipboard roundtrip
./ftui-harness --test-mode clipboard --text "Hello, World!"
# Assert: get() returns same text

# Test with large content
./ftui-harness --test-mode clipboard --size 100KB
# Assert: content preserved

# Test fallback chain
FTUI_CLIPBOARD_BACKEND=none ./ftui-harness --test-mode clipboard --expect-fallback
```

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] OSC 52 implementation works in modern terminals
- [ ] Platform fallback (macOS, Linux X11, Linux Wayland, Windows)
- [ ] Auto-detection of best method
- [ ] Clear error messages for unsupported environments
- [ ] Works in SSH sessions (OSC 52 preferred)
- [ ] Binary content base64 encoded
- [ ] All tests pass","notes":"## Important: Timeout Handling for OSC 52

OSC 52 clipboard read requires careful timeout handling:

```rust
impl Clipboard {
    /// Read from clipboard with timeout
    /// OSC 52 responses may not arrive or be slow
    pub fn get_with_timeout(&self, timeout: Duration) -> Result<String, ClipboardError> {
        match self.backend {
            ClipboardBackend::Osc52 => {
                // Send OSC 52 query
                self.send_osc52_query()?;
                
                // Wait for response with timeout
                match self.wait_for_response(timeout) {
                    Ok(data) => Ok(base64::decode(&data)?),
                    Err(TimeoutError) => {
                        // Fall back to platform clipboard
                        self.fallback_get()
                    }
                }
            }
            _ => self.platform_get(),
        }
    }
}
```

Default timeout: 100ms (configurable)

Also add Timeout variant to error enum:
```rust
pub enum ClipboardError {
    NotAvailable,
    WriteError(String),
    ReadError(String),
    Timeout,  // OSC 52 response didn't arrive
}
```

-{"id":"bd-lff4p","title":"FrankenTerm.WASM: full custom terminal stack (replace crossterm + xterm.js)","description":"Vision
- Build FrankenTerm: a first-party, Rust-first terminal engine and renderer that runs natively and as `frankenterm.wasm`.
- Replace BOTH Crossterm (native) and xterm.js (web) so we control correctness, latency, and UX end-to-end.

North-Star Outcomes
- Web: `ftui-demo-showcase` runs fully client-side inside FrankenTerm at interactive framerates and feels like a native app.
- Web (terminal-class): FrankenTerm can connect to a real PTY bridge and run scripted remote sessions (bidirectional protocol, not just rendering).
- Native: a first-party backend with better lifecycle semantics + better perf than Crossterm.

Hard Constraints (Non-negotiable)
- Safe Rust in-tree (`#![forbid(unsafe_code)]` in our crates; deps may use unsafe internally).
- Determinism where requested: seeded demos, explicit time sources, record/replay.
- Extreme Optimization discipline: baseline/profile/prove/one-lever/gates.
- Accessibility: keyboard-only usability + screen-reader strategy (even with WebGPU/canvas rendering).
- Security for remote mode: explicit threat model, origin/auth controls, no accidental command-exec in prod.

Success Criteria
- Builds:
  - `cargo test` green for workspace
  - `cargo check --target wasm32-unknown-unknown` succeeds for web crates
- Correctness:
  - Golden trace replay gates are green (native + web)
  - Native PTY E2E suite (`tests/e2e/scripts/*`) stays green with detailed JSONL logs
- UX:
  - Web terminal supports: selection+copy/paste, scrollback, OSC 8 hyperlinks, resize storms, search, and a11y modes
- Performance:
  - 120x40 steady-state rendering at 60fps on modern hardware for representative workloads
  - Keystroke-to-photon latency budgets defined and measured for remote sessions

 {"id":"bd-lff4p.12.1","title":"Design: JS/WASM contract for showcase runner (inputs, resize, patches, determinism)","description":"Goal
- Define the exact JS/WASM contract between:
  1) the browser host (HTML+JS),
  2) `FrankenTermWeb` (web terminal surface; `crates/frankenterm-web`), and
  3) the showcase WASM runner (host-driven wrapper around `ftui-demo-showcase` + `ftui-web::StepProgram`).

Why This Is Its Own Bead
- Locking down these formats makes downstream work (runner impl, HTML glue, E2E traces) mechanical and reviewable.

---

Contract v1 (Code-Backed)

1) Terminal Surface (FrankenTermWeb)
- Constructor: `new FrankenTermWeb()`
- Init (async): `await term.init(canvas, options?)`
  - Implemented in `crates/frankenterm-web/src/wasm.rs`.
  - `options` (partial):
    - `cols`/`rows` (u16) default `80x24`
    - `cellWidth`/`cellHeight` (u16) default `8x16`
    - `dpr` (f32) default `1.0`
    - `zoom` (f32) default `1.0`
    - `screenReader|screen_reader`, `highContrast|high_contrast`, `reducedMotion|reduced_motion`, `focused`
    - input encoder features + link open policy + text shaping config (see wasm.rs parsing)

- Geometry / Resize:
  - `term.fitToContainer(containerWidthCssPx: u32, containerHeightCssPx: u32, dpr: f32) -> geometry`
    - returns deterministic snapshot `{ cols, rows, pixelWidth, pixelHeight, cellWidthPx, cellHeightPx, dpr, zoom }`
  - `term.resize(cols: u16, rows: u16)` also exists for direct grid resize.

- Input normalization:
  - `term.input(eventObj)` accepts a normalized, deterministic InputEvent-shaped JS object (NOT a raw DOM event).
  - `term.drainEncodedInputs() -> Array<string>` returns canonical JSON strings (stable for record/replay).
  - `term.drainEncodedInputBytes() -> Array<Uint8Array>` is the VT-bytes path (remote/PTY mode), not used for showcase runner.

- Patch application:
  - `term.applyPatchBatchFlat(spans: Uint32Array, cells: Uint32Array)` is the fast path.

2) Browser Host → FrankenTermWeb InputEvent Object Shape
(Parsed by `parse_input_event()` in `crates/frankenterm-web/src/wasm.rs`.)

Common
- `kind: "key"|"mouse"|"wheel"|"touch"|"composition"|"paste"|"focus"|"accessibility"`
- `mods` may be any of:
  - preferred: `mods: number` (u8 bitset) with SHIFT=1, ALT=2, CTRL=4, SUPER=8
  - or: `mods: { shift, ctrl, alt, super/meta }`
  - or: top-level boolean flags: `shift|shiftKey`, `ctrl|ctrlKey`, `alt|altKey`, `meta|metaKey|super|superKey`

Key
- `{ kind:"key", phase:"down"|"up"|"keydown"|"keyup", key:<DOM key>, code:<DOM code>, repeat?:boolean, mods?:... }`

Mouse (cell coordinates; 0-indexed)
- `{ kind:"mouse", phase:"down"|"up"|"move"|"drag", x:u16, y:u16, button?:u8, mods?:... }`
  - `button` uses: 0=Left, 1=Middle, 2=Right, 3+=Other

Wheel (cell coordinates; deterministic *step* deltas, not pixels)
- `{ kind:"wheel", x:u16, y:u16, dx:i16, dy:i16, mods?:... }`
  - Sign convention matches SGR mouse wheel encoding in `crates/frankenterm-web/src/input.rs`:
    - `dy > 0` = scroll down, `dy < 0` = scroll up
    - `dx > 0` = scroll right, `dx < 0` = scroll left
  - Host MUST quantize DOM wheel input to deterministic i16 steps.
    - Recommended simplest deterministic mapping for the demo: `dx = sign(deltaX)`, `dy = sign(deltaY)` (0 if |delta|==0).

Paste
- `{ kind:"paste", data:string }` (UTF-8; max 786432 bytes enforced)

Focus
- `{ kind:"focus", focused:boolean }`

Composition
- `{ kind:"composition", phase:"start"|"update"|"end"|"cancel"|DOM synonyms, data?:string }`

Touch
- `{ kind:"touch", phase:"start"|"move"|"end"|"cancel", touches:[{id:u32,x:u16,y:u16},...], mods?:... }`

Accessibility
- `{ kind:"accessibility", screenReader?:bool, highContrast?:bool, reducedMotion?:bool, announce?:string }`

3) FrankenTermWeb → Host Encoded Input JSON (drainEncodedInputs)
- Canonical JSON is produced by `InputEvent::to_json_string()` in `crates/frankenterm-web/src/input.rs`.
- It is stable for record/replay (explicit field ordering, compact encoding).
- Shape is `InputEventJson` with `serde(tag="kind", rename_all="snake_case")`.

Examples (illustrative)
- Key: `{ "kind":"key", "phase":"down", "code":"Enter", "mods":0, "repeat":false }`
- Mouse: `{ "kind":"mouse", "phase":"down", "button":0, "x":10, "y":5, "mods":0 }`
- Wheel: `{ "kind":"wheel", "x":10, "y":5, "dx":0, "dy":1, "mods":0 }`
- Paste: `{ "kind":"paste", "data":"hello" }`

4) Encoded Input JSON → Runner → ftui_core::event::Event Mapping
Runner parses each string using the same schema, then converts to `ftui_core::event::Event`.

Canonical mapping (deterministic):
- `kind=key`
  - Map normalized key code (InputEventJson `code`) into `ftui_core::event::KeyCode` (Char/Enter/Escape/...; unknown => KeyCode::Null).
  - `phase=down`:
    - if `repeat=true` => `KeyEventKind::Repeat`
    - else => `KeyEventKind::Press`
  - `phase=up` => `KeyEventKind::Release`
  - `mods` bitset maps 1:1 to `ftui_core::event::Modifiers`.

- `kind=mouse`
  - Map x/y to `Event::Mouse(MouseEvent { x, y, modifiers, kind })`.
  - `phase=move` => `MouseEventKind::Moved`
  - `phase=down/up/drag` uses `button` mapping to `ftui_core::event::MouseButton`.

- `kind=wheel`
  - Map to repeated `Event::Mouse(MouseEventKind::Scroll*)` events.
  - Steps: `steps = max(abs(dx), abs(dy))`, clamped to 0..=16.
  - Direction:
    - if `dy != 0`: `dy > 0 => ScrollDown`, `dy < 0 => ScrollUp`
    - else: `dx > 0 => ScrollRight`, `dx < 0 => ScrollLeft`

- `kind=paste`
  - `Event::Paste(PasteEvent::new(data, /*bracketed=*/false))`

- `kind=focus`
  - `Event::Focus(focused)`

- `kind=composition`
  - `phase=end` with `data` => `Event::Paste(PasteEvent::new(data, /*bracketed=*/false))`
  - other phases => ignored (no-op) for now (still parseable + loggable).

- `kind=touch`
  - For demo compatibility, map to synthetic left-mouse events using the lowest touch id:
    - start => Mouse Down(Left)
    - move => Mouse Drag(Left)
    - end/cancel => Mouse Up(Left)

- `kind=accessibility`
  - Host applies directly to `FrankenTermWeb` via its dedicated APIs (rendering/mirror behavior).
  - Runner may currently ignore, or optionally forward as a custom app message later.

5) Runner → Host Patch ABI (Flat Path)
Runner emits patch batches compatible with `FrankenTermWeb.applyPatchBatchFlat(spans, cells)`.

Source of truth:
- Emission: `ftui-web` (`WebOutputs::flatten_patches_u32()` in `crates/ftui-web/src/lib.rs`)
- Decoding: `cell_patches_from_flat_u32()` in `crates/frankenterm-web/src/renderer.rs`

Invariants
- `spans: Uint32Array` in `[offset, len, offset, len, ...]` order.
  - `offset` is linear row-major cell offset (`row * cols + col`).
  - `len` is measured in CELLS (not u32 words).
  - spans are non-overlapping and ordered by increasing offset.

- `cells: Uint32Array` in `[bg, fg, glyph, attrs, ...]` order.
  - Exactly 4 u32 words per cell.
  - Must be fully consumed by spans (`sum(len) * 4 == cells.length`).

Cell packing
- `bg` / `fg`: packed RGBA as u32 (R in high byte, A in low byte).
- `glyph`: Unicode codepoint (`0` = empty/continuation). Graphemes use a fixed fallback codepoint (see `ftui-web` `cell_to_patch`).
- `attrs`: low 8 bits = style flags, bits 8..31 = hyperlink id.

Empty batches
- Allowed. `spans.length==0 && cells.length==0` means no-op.

6) Resize / Geometry Sync Rules
- The host MUST keep runner size and FrankenTermWeb size in lockstep.
  - After `fitToContainer()` returns `{cols, rows, ...}`, do:
    - `term.resize(cols, rows)` ONLY if you did not call `fitToContainer` (it already updates internal geometry).
    - `runner.resize(cols, rows)` (pushes `Event::Resize`).
- Patch offsets are only meaningful if both sides agree on `cols`.

7) Determinism / Time-Stepping
- Runner uses `ftui-web::StepProgram` deterministic clock (`advance_time(Duration)` / `set_time(Duration)`).
- Two supported host modes:
  1) realtime: per-frame `dt_ms = performance.now() - last_now` (clamped), `advance_time(dt)`, then `step()`.
  2) fixed-step: constant `dt_ms` (e.g. 16) and deterministic seeded replay.

8) JSONL Logging (Recommended; aligns with `e2e-jsonl-v1`)
- Prefer the existing schema used by `frankenterm-web` harness (`crates/frankenterm-web/src/frame_harness.rs`).

Minimum record types for showcase traces:
- `record_type: "showcase_input"`:
  - include: `schema_version`, `timestamp`, `run_id`, `seed`, `event_idx`, and `input_json` (the exact string from `drainEncodedInputs`).
- `record_type: "showcase_step"`:
  - include: `timestamp`, `run_id`, `seed`, `step_idx`, `dt_ms`, `events_processed`, `rendered`, `running`, `frame_idx`, `cols`, `rows`.
  - if rendered: include patch stats + `patch_hash` from `WebOutputs`.
- `record_type: "frame"`:
  - after applying patches, host may call `term.snapshotResizeStormFrameJsonl(run_id, seed, timestamp, frame_idx)` to emit stable frame hashes + geometry for postmortem.

---

Acceptance Criteria
- This description is implementable against current code:
  - `crates/frankenterm-web/src/wasm.rs` (JS surface)
  - `crates/frankenterm-web/src/input.rs` (canonical encoded JSON)
  - `crates/ftui-web/src/step_program.rs` + `crates/ftui-web/src/lib.rs` (runner + patches)
  - `crates/frankenterm-web/src/renderer.rs` (flat patch decoder)

### Architecture Overview

Two WASM objects cooperate; JS orchestrates the data flow:

```
┌──────────────────────────────────────────────┐
│  Browser (JS host loop)                      │
│                                              │
│  FrankenTermWeb          ShowcaseRunner       │
│  (display surface)       (app logic)         │
│  ┌─────────────┐        ┌──────────────┐    │
│  │ WebGPU      │←patches│ StepProgram  │    │
│  │ renderer    │        │ <AppModel>   │    │
│  │ + input     │→inputs │              │    │
│  │ normalizer  │        │              │    │
│  └─────────────┘        └──────────────┘    │
└──────────────────────────────────────────────┘
```

- **FrankenTermWeb** (existing, frankenterm-web crate): owns the WebGPU renderer, input normalization, search, links, accessibility. Accepts cell patches for display.
- **ShowcaseRunner** (new, wasm-bindgen wrapper): wraps `ftui_web::StepProgram<AppModel>`. Processes events, runs the Elm-loop, emits patch batches.
- **JS host**: orchestrates the frame loop, bridges inputs from FrankenTermWeb to ShowcaseRunner, and patches from ShowcaseRunner to FrankenTermWeb.

---

### 1. Inputs Contract

**Flow:** DOM event → FrankenTermWeb.input(eventObj) → normalize → queue → JS drains → ShowcaseRunner.pushEncodedInput(json)

1. Browser captures DOM keyboard/mouse/touch/paste/focus events.
2. JS calls `term.input(eventObj)` where eventObj is an InputEvent-shaped JS object with a `kind` discriminator and cell coordinates.
3. FrankenTermWeb normalizes the event (key-code mapping, modifier tracking, composition handling) and queues a stable JSON encoding.
4. JS drains: `const inputs = term.drainEncodedInputs()` → Array of JSON strings.
5. JS forwards each string to: `runner.pushEncodedInput(jsonStr)` → returns `true` if accepted, `false` if unsupported/malformed.
6. Inside the runner: parse JSON → convert to `ftui_core::event::Event` → `StepProgram::push_event()`.

**JSON input schema** (stable, defined by frankenterm-web `InputEvent::to_json_string()`):

```json
// Key event
{"kind":"key","phase":"down","code":"a","mods":0,"repeat":false}

// Mouse event (cell coordinates)
{"kind":"mouse","phase":"down","button":0,"x":10,"y":5,"mods":0}

// Paste event
{"kind":"paste","data":"hello world"}

// Focus event
{"kind":"focus","focused":true}

// Wheel event
{"kind":"wheel","x":10,"y":5,"dx":0,"dy":-3,"mods":0}
```

**JSON→Event conversion** must live in `ftui-web` (no web-sys dep) as a new function:
```rust
pub fn parse_encoded_input_to_event(json: &str) -> Result<Option<Event>, InputParseError>
```
- Returns `Ok(None)` for events that have no `Event` equivalent (accessibility, composition).
- Returns `Err` for malformed JSON.
- Key mapping: `kind:"key" + phase:"down"` → `Event::Key { code, modifiers, kind: Press }`.
- Mouse mapping: `kind:"mouse"` → `Event::Mouse { kind, x, y, button, modifiers }`.
- Paste mapping: `kind:"paste"` → `Event::Paste`.
- Focus mapping: `kind:"focus"` → `Event::Focus(focused)`.
- Wheel: mapped to `Event::Mouse` with scroll kind, or dropped if no semantic match.

**Invariant:** The runner MUST NOT import web-sys, js-sys, or wasm-bindgen types for input parsing. The JSON parser lives in ftui-web.

---

### 2. Resize Contract

**What the runner consumes:** terminal cols/rows (cell-space) only.

1. Host detects container resize (ResizeObserver, window resize, DPR change).
2. Host calls `term.fitToContainer(widthCss, heightCss, dpr)` → returns `{ cols, rows, pixelWidth, pixelHeight, ... }`.
3. Host calls `runner.resize(cols, rows)` with the returned cell dimensions.
4. Host calls `term.resize(cols, rows)` (if not already done by fitToContainer).

**Lockstep invariant:** FrankenTermWeb and ShowcaseRunner MUST agree on (cols, rows) at all times. The host is responsible for calling both resize methods atomically (before the next frame).

**Pixel size / DPR:** renderer-only concern. The runner receives only cols/rows. The runner internally pushes `Event::Resize { width: cols, height: rows }` which is processed on the next `step()`.

**First frame after resize:** always a full repaint (prev_buffer is invalidated; single span covering all cells).

---

### 3. Patch Contract

After each `runner.step()` where `result.rendered == true`:

1. Host calls `runner.takeFlatPatches()` → returns JS object `{ cells: Uint32Array, spans: Uint32Array }`.
2. `spans` format: `[offset, len, offset, len, ...]`
   - `offset`: linear cell index in row-major order (`y * cols + x`).
   - `len`: number of contiguous cells in this span.
3. `cells` format: `[bg, fg, glyph, attrs, bg, fg, glyph, attrs, ...]`
   - Each cell is 4 × u32 (16 bytes).
   - `bg`: packed RGBA (0xRRGGBBAA).
   - `fg`: packed RGBA.
   - `glyph`: Unicode codepoint (0 = empty, 0x25A1 = grapheme fallback).
   - `attrs`: bits 0-7 = StyleFlags (bold/italic/underline/etc.), bits 8-31 = link_id.
4. Host calls `term.applyPatchBatchFlat(cells, spans)` to update the GPU shadow buffer.
5. Host calls `term.render()` to submit the GPU frame.

**Invariants:**
- First frame after `init()` is always a full repaint: single span `[0, cols*rows]`.
- First frame after `resize()` is always a full repaint.
- Empty patch batches are valid: both `cells` and `spans` are zero-length. This means no visual change; host should skip `applyPatchBatchFlat` + `render`.
- Span ordering: spans are in ascending offset order, non-overlapping.
- The existing `WebOutputs::flatten_patches_u32()` method produces exactly this format.

**For non-rendered steps** (result.rendered == false): host MUST NOT call takeFlatPatches (patches are stale/empty).

---

### 4. Determinism / Time

**Real-time mode** (requestAnimationFrame-driven):
```js
let lastTs = 0;
function frame(timestamp) {
    const dt = lastTs === 0 ? 16 : timestamp - lastTs;
    lastTs = timestamp;
    runner.advanceTime(dt);  // dt in milliseconds (f64)
    // ... drain inputs, step, apply patches ...
    if (runner.isRunning()) requestAnimationFrame(frame);
}
requestAnimationFrame(frame);
```
- `advanceTime(dt_ms: f64)` converts to `Duration::from_secs_f64(dt_ms / 1000.0)` internally.
- Tick events fire when the accumulated time exceeds the model's tick rate.

**Fixed-step replay mode** (for golden traces + CI):
```js
for (const record of trace.records) {
    if (record.event === "input") {
        runner.setTime(record.ts_ns);  // absolute nanoseconds (f64)
        runner.pushEncodedInput(record.payload_json);
    } else if (record.event === "tick") {
        runner.setTime(record.ts_ns);
    } else if (record.event === "resize") {
        runner.setTime(record.ts_ns);
        runner.resize(record.cols, record.rows);
        term.resize(record.cols, record.rows);
    }
    const result = runner.step();
    if (record.event === "frame") {
        assert(runner.patchHash() === record.patch_hash);
    }
}
```
- `setTime(ts_ns: f64)` sets the absolute deterministic clock. ts_ns is nanoseconds since trace start.
- The DeterministicClock inside WebBackend NEVER reads system time.
- Same inputs + same time sequence → identical frame checksums (FNV-1a 64-bit).

**Time injection API:**
- `advanceTime(dt_ms: f64)` — relative, for real-time mode.
- `setTime(ts_ns: f64)` — absolute, for replay mode.
- Both are thin wrappers over StepProgram::advance_time / set_time.

---

### 5. Logging Contract

**Runtime logs:** Model emits `Cmd::Log(text)` → captured by WebPresenter → drained by host.

- `runner.takeLogs()` → Array of strings (log lines from the model).
- Logs are drained (consumed) on each call.

**Host-side JSONL formatting** (for E2E/CI; the runner provides data primitives, host formats):

```jsonl
{"event":"step","frame_idx":1,"ts_ns":16000000,"rendered":true,"events_processed":3}
{"event":"patch_stats","frame_idx":1,"dirty_cells":42,"patch_count":3,"bytes_uploaded":672}
{"event":"frame","frame_idx":1,"patch_hash":"fnv1a64:a1b2c3d4e5f6a7b8"}
{"event":"input","ts_ns":16000000,"kind":"key","code":"a","mods":0}
```

**Runner data accessors** (host reads these after each step):
- `runner.patchHash()` → `Option<String>` (e.g., `"fnv1a64:..."`) — from WebOutputs.
- `runner.patchStats()` → JS object `{ dirty_cells, patch_count, bytes_uploaded }` or `null`.
- `runner.frameIdx()` → u64 (monotonically increasing, starts at 0).

The host is responsible for assembling these into golden-trace-v1 JSONL if recording.

### 6. ShowcaseRunner wasm-bindgen API

```rust
// crate: ftui-showcase-wasm (new, depends on ftui-web + ftui-demo-showcase)
#[wasm_bindgen]
pub struct ShowcaseRunner {
    inner: StepProgram<AppModel>,
}

#[wasm_bindgen]
impl ShowcaseRunner {
    #[wasm_bindgen(constructor)]
    pub fn new(cols: u16, rows: u16) -> Self;

    /// Initialize the model and render first frame. Call exactly once.
    pub fn init(&mut self);

    /// Advance deterministic clock by dt milliseconds (real-time mode).
    #[wasm_bindgen(js_name = advanceTime)]
    pub fn advance_time(&mut self, dt_ms: f64);

    /// Set deterministic clock to absolute nanoseconds (replay mode).
    #[wasm_bindgen(js_name = setTime)]
    pub fn set_time(&mut self, ts_ns: f64);

    /// Parse a JSON-encoded input event and push to the event queue.
    /// Returns true if accepted, false if unsupported/malformed.
    #[wasm_bindgen(js_name = pushEncodedInput)]
    pub fn push_encoded_input(&mut self, json: &str) -> bool;

    /// Resize the terminal (pushes Event::Resize, processed on next step).
    pub fn resize(&mut self, cols: u16, rows: u16);

    /// Process pending events and render if dirty.
    /// Returns { running: bool, rendered: bool, events_processed: u32, frame_idx: u64 }.
    pub fn step(&mut self) -> JsValue;

    /// Take flat patch batch for GPU upload.
    /// Returns { cells: Uint32Array, spans: Uint32Array }.
    /// Only call when step() returned rendered=true.
    #[wasm_bindgen(js_name = takeFlatPatches)]
    pub fn take_flat_patches(&mut self) -> JsValue;

    /// Take accumulated log lines. Returns Array<string>.
    #[wasm_bindgen(js_name = takeLogs)]
    pub fn take_logs(&mut self) -> Array;

    /// FNV-1a hash of the last patch batch, or null.
    #[wasm_bindgen(js_name = patchHash)]
    pub fn patch_hash(&self) -> Option<String>;

    /// Patch upload stats, or null. { dirty_cells, patch_count, bytes_uploaded }.
    #[wasm_bindgen(js_name = patchStats)]
    pub fn patch_stats(&self) -> JsValue;

    /// Current frame index (monotonic).
    #[wasm_bindgen(js_name = frameIdx)]
    pub fn frame_idx(&self) -> u64;

    /// Whether the program is still running.
    #[wasm_bindgen(js_name = isRunning)]
    pub fn is_running(&self) -> bool;

    /// Release resources.
    pub fn destroy(&mut self);
}
```

### 8. Invariants Summary

| Property | Guarantee |
|----------|-----------|
| Determinism | Same inputs + same time → identical patch hashes |
| No system time | DeterministicClock; no Instant::now() in WASM |
| No threads | StepProgram runs synchronously; Cmd::Task executes inline |
| Lockstep geometry | Host calls resize on both FrankenTermWeb and ShowcaseRunner |
| Patch ordering | Spans in ascending offset order, non-overlapping |
| First frame | Always full repaint (single span, all cells) |
| Empty batches | Valid; host skips render |
| Input drop | Unknown kinds return false from pushEncodedInput; not an error |

 {"id":"bd-lff4p.12.3","title":"Implement WASM showcase runner (wasm-bindgen wrapper around ftui-web::StepProgram<AppModel>)","description":"Goal
- Expose the `ftui-demo-showcase` application model as a WASM-callable, host-driven runner that can be stepped from JS and emits patch batches for `FrankenTermWeb`.

Key Requirement
- The runner must be *browser-hosted* (no crossterm, no terminal I/O), and must not pull in heavy web-only dependencies into core crates.

Implementation Options (pick one and document why)
1) Add a `wasm`/`web` feature to `ftui-demo-showcase` and export a `#[wasm_bindgen]` API from that crate.
2) Create a minimal new crate (preferred if we want a clean boundary): `crates/ftui-demo-showcase-web` with `cdylib` + wasm-bindgen that depends on `ftui-demo-showcase` + `ftui-web`.
   - Justification: wasm-bindgen is a platform boundary; keeping it out of core crates preserves clean layering.

Runner API Sketch
- `new(options)` / `init(cols, rows, seed, view?)`
- `resize(cols, rows)`
- `applyEncodedInputs(inputs: string[])`
- `step(dt_ms)` OR `stepFixed(frame_idx, dt_ms)`
- `drainPatchBatchFlat() -> { spans: Uint32Array, cells: Uint32Array }` (or multiple batches)
- Optional: `currentViewId()` / `setViewId(...)` for direct navigation (useful for tests).

Determinism
- Runner must support fixed timestep stepping for replay tests.
- Any RNG or time sources used by the showcase must be injectable/seedable in this mode.

Logging
- Emit JSONL lines (to JS or to an in-memory buffer) with frame_idx, dt, view, and patch stats, so headless runs can produce rich artifacts.

Validation
- Unit tests in native Rust where possible for the runner core logic (no web APIs).
- wasm-bindgen tests for the JS-facing surface (smoke + simple interaction).

Dependencies
- Blocked on: bd-lff4p.12.1 (contract) and bd-lff4p.12.2 (ftui-core tracing on wasm).

- Batching: instancing strategy, buffer update strategy (full vs dirty spans), CPU->GPU transfer budgets.

Acceptance Criteria
- Renderer tasks can be implemented without ambiguity.
- Includes a measurable frame budget and how we will profile it.

 {"id":"bd-lff4p.2.10","title":"Bench: web renderer frame-time harness + regression thresholds","description":"Goal
- Build a repeatable web performance harness for FrankenTerm that supports the Extreme Optimization loop.

Metrics (minimum)
- Frame time histogram (p50/p95/p99) at 80x24 and 120x40.
- Time-to-first-frame and time-to-interactive.
- Patch statistics: bytes per frame, dirty-span counts, draw-call counts.
- Memory usage (JS + WASM) steady-state and after stress (scrollback/resize).
- GPU timings when available (best-effort).

Outputs
- Machine-readable JSON for CI gating and local profiling.
- Optional JSONL stream for per-frame/per-event diagnostics (run_id, git_sha, browser, DPR).

Acceptance Criteria
- Baseline runs are stable enough to detect regressions (document expected variance + thresholds).
- Harness can be driven by deterministic traces (record/replay) so profiles are comparable.
